claim.hypotheses.explanation: Includes the correct hypothesis (women more concerned than men) but adds unrelated hypotheses not in the reference.

claim.hypotheses_location.explanation: Matches the reference: abstract and Summary section (p. 10).

claim.statement.explanation: Extracted statement focuses on age-related risk estimation, whereas the reference statement concerns gender differences in worry with specific percentages.

claim.statement_location.explanation: Extracted locates the statement in the Abstract/Summary; the reference statement is in the Fear section on p. 5.

claim.study_type.explanation: Both classify the study as Observational.

data.source.explanation: Both indicate an ISO-certified online access panel provider as the source.

data.wave_or_subset.explanation: Both note data collection from March 19–23, 2020; extracted adds sample cleaning details without contradicting the reference.

data.unit_of_analysis.explanation: Both identify adult respondents; extracted provides additional age range context consistent with the study.

data.access_details.explanation: Consistent with reference: panel provider handled data; no public access process stated.

data.notes.explanation: Extracted provides relevant caveats (mandatory items, speeders removed) which are consistent with the paper; the reference did not specify notes.

method.description.explanation: Accurately summarizes the survey and analyses; aligns with the reference focus on concern/fear/coping and subgroup differences.

method.steps.explanation: Contains the core steps (collection, cleaning, descriptives) consistent with the reference; adds detail without contradiction.

method.models.explanation: Reference suggests chi-squared tests, whereas extracted mentions t-tests and correlations; both indicate simple descriptive/group comparisons but not the same model.

method.outcome_variable.explanation: Includes worry about COVID-19 among several outcomes; broader than the reference which focuses on worry.

method.independent_variables.explanation: Includes gender (matches reference) plus additional variables (age, region, urbanicity).

method.control_variables.explanation: Reference indicates NA; extracted states not stated—equivalent.

results.summary.explanation: Includes the key finding that women are more worried than men (consistent with reference) but adds many other findings beyond the referenced result.

results.numerical_results[0].outcome_nam.explanation: Refers to perceived likelihood mean, whereas the reference outcome is worry about COVID-19 by gender.

results.numerical_results[0].value.explanation: Provides a Likert mean (2.87) instead of the reference percentages (women 68.2%, men 55.7%).

results.numerical_results[0].unit.explanation: Uses a 5-point Likert scale; reference reports percentages.

results.numerical_results[0].statistical.explanation: Not stated vs. reference indicates significance (true).

results.numerical_results[0].direction.explanation: Direction provided does not correspond to the reference (which does not specify direction for this metric).

results.numerical_results[1].outcome_nam.explanation: Different outcome (family/friends risk) than reference (worry by gender).

results.numerical_results[1].statistical.explanation: Not stated vs. reference indicates significance.

results.numerical_results[1].direction.explanation: Direction does not correspond to the reference metric.

results.numerical_results[2].outcome_nam.explanation: Different outcome (general infection likelihood) than reference (worry by gender).

results.numerical_results[2].statistical.explanation: Not stated vs. reference indicates significance.

results.numerical_results[2].direction.explanation: Direction does not correspond to the reference metric.

results.numerical_results[3].outcome_nam.explanation: Behavior change (hand washing) differs from reference (worry by gender).

results.numerical_results[3].unit.explanation: Both use percentage units, but the metric differs.

results.numerical_results[3].statistical.explanation: Marked true without corresponding reference support.

results.numerical_results[3].direction.explanation: Direction not applicable to the reference metric.
